title: Prompting for Effective LLM Reasoning and Planning (Udacity + Microsoft)
description: 12-hour intermediate course covering Chain-of-Thought, ReAct prompting, role-based prompting, prompt chaining for agentic reasoning, LLM feedback loops, and multi-agent workflows using Azure AI Foundry Agent Service.

title: Advanced Prompt Engineering: CoT, COSTAR and XML (FindSkill)
description: Covers structured prompting with XML tags and JSON schemas, COSTAR framework, Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, Self-Consistency, few-shot mastery, system prompt design, output control, and prompt security.

title: Mastering Prompt Engineering with CoT, ReAct, and ToT (Cognixia)
description: 3-day hands-on intensive covering CoT for arithmetic/logic/finance/healthcare/code, ReAct framework with external tools and RAG integration, Tree-of-Thought for coding/planning/strategy, and evaluation frameworks.

title: Prompt Engineering 2026: Demystifying Advanced AI Techniques (Sikho)
description: 120-hour self-paced course covering CoT, ToT, self-consistency, self-correction, RAG, meta-prompting, role-based constraints, agentic AI systems, and future trends including automated prompt generation and multimodal prompting.

title: Advanced Prompt Engineering (Educative)
description: Covers least-to-most prompting, generated knowledge, PAL (Program-Aided Language), APE (Automatic Prompt Engineer), and combining techniques like Least-to-most + CoT, PAL + prompt chaining.

title: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Interactive Tutorial
description: Official Anthropic tutorial covering Claude-specific prompting best practices: being clear and direct, using examples, giving Claude a role, using XML tags, and leveraging Claude strengths.

title: OpenAI Prompt Engineering Guide
description: Official OpenAI guide covering 6 strategies: write clear instructions, provide reference text, split complex tasks, give time to think, use external tools, and test systematically.

title: Google Prompt Engineering for Developers (DeepLearning.AI)
description: Andrew Ng and Isa Fulford teach practical prompt engineering for software developers: summarization, inference, transformation, expansion, chatbot building with the OpenAI API.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Workflow
description: Step-by-step approach to crafting effective CoT prompts that elicit reasoning.
workflow: State the problem clearly and specify the format
Add Think step by step or Let us think step by step to encourage reasoning
Provide 2-3 few-shot examples showing the reasoning chain
Structure the desired output format explicitly
Test on diverse inputs and verify reasoning quality
Iterate: refine the prompt if reasoning goes off track
Add verification: Double-check your answer for accuracy

title: System Prompt Design Pattern
description: Structured approach to designing effective system prompts for production LLM applications.
workflow: Define the role: who is the AI?
Set behavioral constraints: what it must/must not do
Specify output format: JSON, markdown, or specific schema
Add knowledge boundaries: what it knows, what it does not
Include error handling instructions: what to do when unsure
Set tone and style guidelines
Add few-shot examples for edge cases
Include self-evaluation: Rate your confidence in this answer

title: ReAct (Reason+Act) Prompting Pattern
description: Iterative reasoning-action loop prompting pattern for tool-using agents.
workflow: Define available tools and their descriptions in the system prompt
Instruct the model to follow Thought-Action-Observation loop
Thought: model reasons about what to do next
Action: model selects a tool and provides input
Observation: system returns tool output
Model processes observation and decides next step
Repeat until model provides final answer
Add guardrails: max iterations, error handling, fallback responses

title: Few-Shot Prompt Engineering Process
description: Systematic process for creating and testing few-shot prompts with optimal example selection.
workflow: Collect 20+ input-output examples of the target task
Select 3-5 diverse examples covering edge cases and common cases
Order examples from simple to complex
Format examples consistently with clear input/output delimiters
Add a format explanation: Here are examples of X to Y mappings
Test on held-out examples and measure accuracy
Iterate: replace low-performing examples with better ones
Consider using dynamic few-shot: retrieve relevant examples per query

title: Structured Output with JSON Schema
description: Ensuring LLM outputs conform to a specific schema using prompt engineering and validation.
workflow: Define your output schema as a JSON Schema
Include the schema in the system prompt with an example
Use the model structured output feature if available (OpenAI, Anthropic)
Add a retry loop that re-prompts on schema validation failure
Implement Pydantic or Zod validation on the receiving end
Log validation failure rates for prompt improvement
Add fallback: if 3 retries fail, return a default/error response
Monitor schema conformance rate in production

title: Chain-of-Thought with Self-Verification
description: Prompt template that asks the model to reason step-by-step and then verify its own answer.
code:
SYSTEM_PROMPT = """
You are a mathematical reasoning assistant.
For every problem:
1. Read the problem carefully
2. Think step by step through your solution
3. Show all intermediate calculations
4. After arriving at your answer, verify it by working backwards
5. If verification fails, re-examine your steps
6. Provide your final answer in the format: ANSWER: [number]

Problem: {problem}
"""

title: ReAct Agent Prompt Template
description: System prompt for a ReAct-style agent that reasons and uses tools iteratively.
code:
REACT_PROMPT = """
You are a helpful assistant with access to the following tools:

{tool_descriptions}

Use the following format:

Thought: I need to figure out...
Action: [tool_name](input)
Observation: [tool result]
... (repeat Thought/Action/Observation as needed)
Thought: I now know the final answer
Final Answer: [your response]

When you don't need a tool, respond directly with:
Final Answer: [your response]
"""

title: Structured JSON Output Prompt
description: System prompt that forces the model to output valid JSON matching a specific schema.
code:
STRUCTURED_PROMPT = """
You are a data extraction assistant. Extract information from the text
and output ONLY valid JSON matching this schema:

{
  "name": "string - full name",
  "email": "string - email address or null",
  "sentiment": "string - one of: positive, negative, neutral",
  "key_topics": ["string - list of main topics"],
  "confidence": "number - 0.0 to 1.0"
}

Example output:
{
  "name": "Jane Smith",
  "email": "jane@example.com",
  "sentiment": "positive",
  "key_topics": ["product launch", "revenue growth"],
  "confidence": 0.92
}

IMPORTANT: Output ONLY the JSON object, no additional text.
"""

title: Meta-Prompt for Automatic Prompt Optimization
description: A prompt that generates improved prompts based on task description and evaluation criteria.
code:
META_PROMPT = """
You are a prompt engineer. Given a task description and evaluation
criteria, generate the optimal prompt.

Task: {task_description}
Evaluation Criteria: {criteria}

Generate a prompt that:
1. Clearly defines the task and expected output format
2. Includes relevant few-shot examples
3. Specifies constraints and edge case handling
4. Maximizes the evaluation criteria score
5. Is concise and unambiguous

Output the improved prompt in a code block.
"""

title: Multi-Turn Conversation with Context Management
description: System prompt pattern for managing long conversations with context window limits.
code:
CONVERSATION_PROMPT = """
You are a helpful assistant in an ongoing conversation.

BEHAVIOR RULES:
- Stay on topic and be concise
- If the user asks something unrelated, politely redirect
- Never reveal you are an AI unless directly asked

CONTEXT MANAGEMENT:
- You will receive a summary of previous conversation turns
- Reference specific details from the conversation when relevant
- If you are unsure about something mentioned earlier, ask for clarification

OUTPUT FORMAT:
- Use markdown for structured responses
- Include relevant headers for long responses
- Bold key terms for emphasis

Previous conversation summary:
{conversation_summary}
"""

title: Promptfoo
description: Open-source CLI tool for testing and evaluating LLM prompts across multiple models. Supports assertion-based testing, regression detection, and comparison of prompt variations.

title: DSPy
description: Programming framework for LLMs that algorithmically optimizes prompts. Define signatures (input/output schemas), build modules (CoT, ReAct, multi-hop), and let DSPy find optimal prompts automatically.

title: LangSmith Prompt Playground
description: Interactive playground for testing, iterating, and versioning prompts. Supports side-by-side comparison, A/B testing, and dataset-driven evaluation.

title: Anthropic Workbench
description: Official Anthropic prompt engineering workbench for designing, testing, and iterating on prompts with Claude. Includes prompt templates, examples, and evaluation tools.

title: OpenAI Playground
description: Official OpenAI playground for experimenting with prompts, adjusting parameters (temperature, top_p, frequency penalty), and comparing model responses side by side.

title: Least-to-Most Prompting
description: Decompose complex problems into simpler sub-problems, solving each sequentially. Reduces reasoning errors on multi-step tasks by 30-50% compared to direct prompting.

title: Self-Consistency Decoding
description: Sample multiple reasoning paths for the same prompt, then select the most consistent answer by majority vote. Improves accuracy on arithmetic and reasoning tasks by 5-15%.

title: Directional Stimulus Prompting
description: Use a separate hint model to generate directional stimulus (guidance tokens) that steer the main model toward desired output qualities. Reduces hallucination and improves factuality.

title: Prompt Chaining Pattern
description: Decompose a complex task into a sequence of focused prompts, where each prompt processes the output of the previous one. Reduces hallucination and improves quality on complex tasks.

title: Emotional Prompting
description: Adding emotional context (This is very important to my career or Take a deep breath) to prompts can improve model performance on reasoning and generation tasks by 5-10%.

title: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Guide
description: Official guide with 6 key strategies: be clear and direct, use examples, give Claude a role, use XML tags, show Claude its work, and give long context tasks with care.

title: Learn Prompting (Open-Source Course)
description: Free, comprehensive open-source course covering prompt engineering from basics to advanced: CoT, ToT, ReAct, few-shot, self-consistency, prompt hacking, and more. Community-driven with 50+ lessons.

title: Prompt Engineering Guide (DAIR.AI)
description: Community-maintained guide with techniques, models, and papers. Covers CoT, ToT, self-consistency, ReAct, PAL, and more. Includes interactive examples and model comparisons.

title: Microsoft Prompt Engineering Guide
description: Official Microsoft guide covering system message design, few-shot examples, chain-of-thought, and best practices for Azure OpenAI Service deployments.

title: The Art of Prompt Engineering (OpenAI Guide)
description: Comprehensive guide from OpenAI covering prompt design principles, iterative refinement, common pitfalls, and advanced techniques for GPT models.

title: Prompt Engineering for Generative AI (O'Reilly)
description: Deep dive into prompt engineering for text, image, and code generation. Covers structured prompts, system prompts, prompt chaining, and evaluation methods.

title: Prompt Engineering for LLMs (Cohere)
description: Official Cohere course covering prompt design, few-shot learning, chain-of-thought, and production prompt patterns for Command R+ models.

title: IBM Prompt Engineering for Generative AI
description: Covers prompt patterns for IBM watsonx, retrieval-augmented generation, structured output, and enterprise prompt governance.

title: Automatic Prompt Optimization with DSPy
description: Use DSPy to algorithmically optimize prompts through bootstrapping and Bayesian search.
workflow: Define your task as a DSPy Signature: input and output fields
Create a metric function that scores prompt quality
Build a training set with 50-200 input/output examples
Choose an optimizer: BootstrapFewShot, MIPROv2, or BootstrapFewShotWithRandomSearch
Compile the module: optimized_module = optimizer.compile(module, trainset=train_data)
Evaluate on held-out test set and compare with baseline
Iterate: adjust optimizer hyperparameters if quality is insufficient
Deploy the optimized prompts to production
Monitor drift and re-optimize periodically

title: Production Prompt Testing Pipeline
description: Systematic pipeline for testing prompts before deploying to production.
workflow: Create a test dataset with 100+ input/output pairs covering edge cases
Define evaluation metrics: accuracy, relevance, safety, format compliance
Run baseline prompt against test dataset and record scores
Iterate prompt variations: change instructions, add examples, adjust format
Run A/B tests comparing prompt variations on the same dataset
Use majority voting or self-consistency for high-stakes decisions
Set up regression testing: run test suite on every prompt change
Deploy canary: route 5% of traffic to new prompt, monitor metrics
Roll out if canary metrics are within acceptable range
Set up continuous monitoring for drift and degradation

title: XML-Structured Prompt for Data Extraction
description: System prompt using XML tags to structure extraction tasks with clear delimiters.
code:
EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """
You are a data extraction assistant. Extract information from the provided text.

<instructions>
- Extract each field listed in <output_schema>
- If a field is not found, output null
- Do not infer or fabricate information
- Preserve original text for extracted values
</instructions>

<output_schema>
{
  "company_name": "string",
  "revenue": "string - with currency symbol",
  "fiscal_year": "string - YYYY format",
  "key_products": ["string"],
  "sentiment": "positive | negative | neutral"
}
</output_schema>

<text>
{input_text}
</text>

Output ONLY valid JSON matching the schema above.
"""

title: Role-Based Prompt for Code Review
description: System prompt that assigns a specific expert role for code review tasks.
code:
CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT = """
You are a senior software engineer with 15 years of experience in:
- Distributed systems and microservices architecture
- Performance optimization and profiling
- Security best practices (OWASP Top 10)
- Code maintainability and clean code principles

<review_criteria>
1. CORRECTNESS: Does the code do what it claims? Any bugs?
2. SECURITY: Any injection, auth, or data exposure risks?
3. PERFORMANCE: N+1 queries? Unnecessary allocations? Caching opportunities?
4. MAINTAINABILITY: Clear naming? Proper abstractions? Missing tests?
5. SCALABILITY: Will this work at 10x, 100x current load?
</review_criteria>

<output_format>
For each issue found:
- SEVERITY: critical | warning | suggestion
- CATEGORY: correctness | security | performance | maintainability | scalability
- LOCATION: file:line
- DESCRIPTION: what is wrong and why
- SUGGESTION: specific fix with code example
</output_format>
"""

title: Iterative Refinement Prompt Pattern
description: System prompt for generating and iteratively refining output through self-critique.
code:
REFINEMENT_PROMPT = """
You are an expert writer. Your task has three phases:

Phase 1 - DRAFT:
Generate an initial response based on the user's request.
Be creative and comprehensive.

Phase 2 - CRITIQUE:
Review your draft critically. Identify:
- Factual errors or unsupported claims
- Logical inconsistencies
- Missing important points
- Unclear or ambiguous language
- Tone or style issues

Phase 3 - REVISE:
Rewrite the response addressing all critique points.
Improve clarity, accuracy, and completeness.

Format your output as:
<DRAFT>...
</DRAFT>
<CRITIQUE>...
</CRITIQUE>
<REVISED>...
</REVISED>
"""

title: PromptLayer
description: Platform for tracking, debugging, and optimizing LLM prompts in production. Provides prompt versioning, A/B testing, cost tracking, and performance analytics.

title: Guardrails AI
description: Open-source framework for adding programmable guardrails to LLM applications. Validates input/output against schemas, prevents hallucination, and enforces safety policies.

title: Outlines
description: Open-source library for generating structured text from LLMs. Guarantees output conforms to JSON schemas, regular expressions, or Pydantic models using constrained decoding.

title: Program-Aided Language (PAL) Pattern
description: Use LLMs to generate code that solves reasoning problems, then execute the code for accurate results. Eliminates arithmetic errors and improves reasoning accuracy by 20-40%.

title: Tree-of-Thought Prompting
description: Explore multiple reasoning paths in parallel, evaluate each, and backtrack from poor paths. Outperforms CoT on complex planning and creative tasks by 25-50%.

title: Self-Ask Prompting Pattern
description: Model decomposes complex questions into sub-questions, answers each, then combines answers. Particularly effective for multi-hop reasoning and knowledge-intensive tasks.

title: Instruction Following Hierarchy Pattern
description: Use a priority hierarchy in system prompts: safety > task > format > style. This prevents instruction conflicts and ensures consistent behavior across diverse inputs.

title: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Cookbook
description: Collection of practical prompt engineering examples and techniques from Anthropic, including tool use, vision, and complex reasoning patterns.

title: LangChain Prompt Templates Guide
description: Official guide for creating reusable prompt templates with LangChain, including ChatPromptTemplate, few-shot templates, and output parsing.

title: ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers (DeepLearning.AI)
description: Andrew Ng and Isa Fulford teach practical prompt engineering for software developers: summarization, inference, transformation, expansion, and chatbot building.

title: Prompt Engineering Complete Guide: CoT, DSPy, Structured Output (youngju.dev)
description: Extremely comprehensive guide covering zero-shot through CoT, ToT, Self-Consistency, ReAct, DSPy automatic optimization, Pydantic structured output, XML with Claude, Function Calling, model-specific optimization (GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini 2.0, Llama 3), Constitutional AI, Meta-Prompting, and prompt injection defense. Full Python code examples.

title: Advanced LLM Prompt Engineering: CoT, ReAct, and Tree of Thoughts (youngju.dev)
description: Deep dive into CoT variants (zero-shot, few-shot, Auto-CoT, Layered CoT), ReAct with Wikipedia search tool, Self-Consistency sampling, ToT BFS implementation, structured output (JSON Mode + Pydantic, Function Calling), production prompt management (versioning, A/B testing, guardrails).

title: Every Prompt Engineering Technique Explained: The Research-Backed Guide (SurePrompts)
description: 12 techniques with research citations and benchmark numbers from original papers (ToT: 4% to 74% on Game of 24, Self-Consistency: +17.9% on GSM8K, ReAct: +34% on ALFWorld). Includes decision framework, technique stacking recommendations, and a prompt builder tool.

title: Prompt Engineering 2026: The Frameworks That Actually Work (Pasquale Pillitteri)
description: Framework-focused guide covering RTF, TAG, TASS, BAB, CARE, CREATE, COAST plus scientific techniques (CoT, ToT, ReAct, Self-Refine). Covers the shift from Prompt Engineering to Context Engineering. Includes A/B test data (CREATE+CoT: 64% reply rate vs 19% basic).

title: Promptify
description: Prompt engineering and versioning library for getting structured output from GPT and other prompt-based models. 4.6k+ stars. Handles prompt templates, versioning, and output parsing.
